title: sanguisorba minor subsp. rupicola
intro: Sanguisorba minor subsp. rupicola, also known as alpine burnet, is a subspecies of Sanguisorba minor, a perennial plant in the rose family native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which are attributed to its high content of tannins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Sanguisorba minor subsp. rupicola include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate digestive issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including cuts and wounds, diarrhea, and menstrual disorders.
